name: Valle de Allín-Metauten – circular por Artavia y La Chopera
region_intro: Mountain biking around Metauten offers diverse terrain in the Navarra region of Spain. The area is characterized by the Sierra de Lókiz to the north and west, featuring rocky escarpments and elevations up to 1,025 meters. Valleys with varied vegetation, including holm oaks and junipers, descend towards the Ega River. The nearby Urbasa and Andía Natural Park adds extensive beech forests and clear rivers to the landscape. Best mountain bike trails around Metauten The most popular mountain bike trail is Ancín Freeland Enduro Loop, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route features technical sections and significant descents. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Lokiz – circular from Ganuza, a difficult 30.8 miles (49.6 km) path. This route explores the challenging topography of the Sierra de Lókiz, offering panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Valle de Allín-Metauten – circular por Artavia y La Chopera, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) trail leading through varied valley landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Metauten is defined by rocky escarpments, diverse forests, and river valleys. The extensive network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Metauten are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Metauten's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Metauten?
A: There are over 200 mountain bike trails around Metauten, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 205 routes in the area.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Metauten?
A: Metauten offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails. You'll find approximately 26 easy routes, 131 moderate routes, and 48 difficult routes, catering to beginners, intermediate riders, and experienced mountain bikers alike.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Metauten?
A: Yes, Metauten has several easy mountain bike trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Lokiz – Sunset View of Zufia loop from Murieta&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 23.9 km route offering pleasant riding with manageable elevation changes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Metauten?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988086435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Lokiz – circular from Ganuza&lt;/a&gt; offer difficult terrain, significant elevation gains (over 1600 meters), and technical sections across nearly 50 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988019338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Allín-Metauten – circular por Artavia y La Chopera&lt;/a&gt;, a 41.3 km difficult trail through varied valley landscapes.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I explore while mountain biking in Metauten?
A: The Metauten area is rich in natural beauty. You can ride through the Sierra de Lókiz, known for its rocky escarpments and panoramic views from peaks like Sardegi. The nearby Urbasa and Andía Natural Park features one of the largest beech forests on the Iberian Peninsula and is home to the stunning Urederra River Spring, famous for its clear, turquoise waters. You can also find various mountain passes and summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3711218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Piedramillera&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cipriano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites near the mountain bike trails in Metauten?
A: Yes, beyond its natural trails, Metauten offers cultural points of interest. You can visit the Black Truffle Museum, which highlights the region's natural truffle growth. The area is also close to the historic Iranzu Monastery and the medieval city of Estella, which is an important stop on the Camino de Santiago. Some routes even pass by historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6931774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road and Portillo de Piedramillera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Metauten?
A: The mountain biking experience in Metauten is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, which includes rocky escarpments, varied forests, and scenic river valleys, offering a mix of technical challenges and beautiful landscapes.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Metauten?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Metauten are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988086435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Lokiz – circular from Ganuza&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988019338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Allín-Metauten – circular por Artavia y La Chopera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Metauten?
A: Metauten benefits from a Mediterranean-influenced climate, making it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summer can be warm, but the diverse vegetation and proximity to natural parks like Urbasa and Andía provide shaded sections and cooler microclimates.

Q: Can I find water sources along the mountain bike trails?
A: Some of the more extensive routes in the Metauten area, particularly those suitable for longer rides, are known to have water sources available. For example, the challenging 'Ancín - Metauten - Ancín' route, which covers significant distances, includes convenient water points along its course.

Q: Are there any specific areas for enduro mountain biking in Metauten?
A: Yes, the Metauten area is recognized for its enduro mountain biking opportunities. The "Arteaga-Metauten Multi Trail" specifically highlights enduro riding. A popular route for this style is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988103282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancín Freeland Enduro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 30.7 km trail featuring technical sections and significant descents.
tips: Very close to the town of Zubielqui is this gravel track that goes into the valley and offers beautiful views towards the Belastegi (NE) and towards the Sartzaleta massif (NW).
